prompt:
Our badge readers push fixed-size binary records over serial and the vendor 'SDK' for decoding them is a 32-bit Windows DLL, so we're reimplementing the wire format in Python. Module binrec.py. And do it with actual byte handling — int arithmetic, bytes/bytearray — NOT the struct module; the whole reason this ticket exists is that nobody on the team can debug the current struct format-string soup.

Schema(fields) takes a list of (name, type) tuples and validates hard (SchemaError): types are uint8/uint16/uint32, strN for any N >= 1 (str8, str16, ...), and flag; names must be non-empty and unique; an empty field list is meaningless. Expose schema.size, the fixed record length in bytes.

Wire layout, in declaration order: uints big-endian; strN is exactly N bytes, the value UTF-8-encoded then NUL-padded to N. Each run of CONSECUTIVE flag fields shares bytes — 8 per byte, first flag of the run in bit 0 (LSB) of the first byte, ceil(n/8) bytes total; any non-flag field ends the run and the next flag starts a fresh byte.

pack(record) -> bytes, strict (PackError, and name the offending field in the message): every field present, no extra keys, uints must be real ints in range (bools do NOT count as ints here, and floats don't either), flags must be real bools (0/1 are rejected — we've been burned), strings must be str, fit in N bytes once encoded, and contain no NUL (NUL is reserved for padding).

unpack(data) -> dict, also strict (UnpackError): exact length only, strings strip their NUL padding and must decode as valid UTF-8, and any set bit in a flag byte beyond that run's flags means a corrupt record — reject it, don't shrug.

SchemaError, PackError, UnpackError should all subclass ValueError so existing call sites can catch broadly. test_binrec.py is the acceptance contract — python3 test_binrec.py must pass unmodified. Stdlib only, struct excluded.
